data.body:
## Description

**What**: Create a test fixture with a known git diff and expected documentation output, and run a dogfooding test on the most recently completed epic.
**Why**: Validates that the doc-writer agent produces correct, non-hallucinated output before the epic is closed. The test fixture provides repeatable mechanical validation; the dogfooding test validates real-world behavior.
**Scope**:
- IN: Test fixture with known diff → expected output; dogfooding run on most recent completed epic; structured comparison recorded in plugins/dso/docs/designs/doc-writer-dogfood-result.md
- OUT: Agent creation (Story 9e22-2d3d)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a test fixture with a known git diff produces output matching expected tier placement (zero incorrectly placed files) and no hallucinated cross-references
  ← Satisfies: "A test fixture exists with a known git diff and expected documentation output"
- When this story is complete, the agent has been run on the most recently completed epic and the comparison is recorded in plugins/dso/docs/designs/doc-writer-dogfood-result.md with pass/fail on three dimensions: changed-section identification accuracy, tier placement correctness, absence of hallucinated references
  ← Satisfies: "The agent is run on the most recently completed epic as a dogfooding test"

## Considerations

- [Testing] Dogfooding test depends on availability of a completed epic with manual doc commits accessible from git history — ensure baseline commits are identifiable
